query: Create a new Python repo for me. I'm building a CLI for parsing Slack export JSON files. Owner: 'datapipe-corp', name: 'slack-export-tools'.
Can you make a repo for this python CLI I want to build? It's a tool for converting markdown tables to CSV. Personal account, repo name 'md2csv'.
Create a new Python CLI for me — should be a fresh repo. Going to parse RSS feeds and dedupe entries. Call it 'feed-dedup'.
Spin up a new python project. I want to build a small REST API for serving model predictions. Name 'inference-gateway'.
Can you create a UV project for me? Working title: 'pgvector-playground', owner 'alice-research'. I'll be experimenting with embedding databases.
Scaffold a new Python package — I want to wrap the OpenAI API with retry logic. Repo name 'oai-retry-wrapper', personal account.
Make me a fresh python repo, name 'tz-shifter'. It'll be a CLI for converting between time zones with DST handling.
ok let's create a new python project — small thing that monitors my Hue lights via the local bridge API. Personal account, 'hue-watcher'.
I need a new Python repo for processing CSV files into parquet. Should run as a CLI, name 'csv2parquet', under my org 'analytics-team'.
Help me start a new python project — going to be a webhook receiver for github events that posts to slack. Name 'gh2slack', org 'platform'.
Add a new Python module to my existing project for handling Redis caching. The project is in ~/projects/web-api and I want the module at app/cache.py.
Convert this Python script into a proper class structure with type hints. Currently it's all functions in main.py.
Fix the bug in my Python project's auth module — JWT validation is rejecting tokens that should be valid. The token contains an aud claim with a list of audiences.
What's the difference between Poetry and uv for Python dependency management? I'm trying to decide which to standardize on for our team.
Show me how to write a Python decorator that times function calls and emits the duration to stdout in microseconds.
Help me debug why my Python tests are failing. The test passes individually but fails when the whole pytest suite runs — looks like state is leaking between tests.
I'm building a Rust CLI, can you scaffold a Cargo project? It's going to be a TUI for monitoring kubernetes pods.
Create a new Node.js repo for me. Going to build a discord bot, name 'mod-helper', using TypeScript and bun.
Optimize this Python function for memory usage. It currently loads the whole CSV into memory; should stream row by row.
Run pytest in my Python project and tell me which tests are failing. Project is at ~/projects/analytics-cli.
